items.headline: Rumfield is one of MLB's top rookies thanks to Minor League lessons

news_items.headline: Devers apologized to Vitello: 'A misunderstanding'...
news_items.summary: Giants first baseman Rafael Devers apologized to manager Tony Vitello and said his actions at first base in the ninth inning of San Francisco's one-run loss to Miami on Sunday are being "blown out of proportion."

news_items.headline: Cardinals elevate DeWitt to chief executive office...
news_items.summary: Bill DeWitt III was named chief executive officer of the St. Louis Cardinals on Wednesday after serving as team president since 2008.

news_items.headline: Mets move scuffling Senga to bullpen after loss
news_items.summary: Mets manager Carlos Mendoza said right-hander Kodai Senga has been demoted to the bullpen and did not rule out of the possibility of left fielder Juan Soto going on the injured list after he exited Tuesday's game with back tightness.

news_items.headline: Cubs lose two starters as Brown, Cabrera to IL
news_items.summary: Cubs right-hander Ben Brown is joining fellow righty Edward Cabrera on the sideline, as both pitchers have been placed on the 15-day injured list.

news_items.headline: Athletics' Gelof exits after hand spiked by cleat
news_items.summary: Athletics infielder Zack Gelof left Tuesday night's game in the second inning with a gruesome injury after the Giants' Matt Chapman stepped on Gelof's right hand during an awkward tag play at second base.

news_items.headline: Cubs' Counsell perplexed by rainout in New York
news_items.summary: After his team got rained out two days in a row, Cubs manager Craig Counsell sounded a little miffed about the second one in New York. Hours later Tuesday, he was criticizing a major league rule that led to his ejection following an unusual play.

news_items.headline: Stearns: Still time for Mets to contend this seaso...
news_items.summary: Mets president of baseball operations David Stearns said he views the Aug. 3 trade deadline as the "cut off" to decide whether to push for the playoffs or shift their focus to 2027.

news_items.headline: In failing to address Pride Night controversy, Giants' Buster Posey risks tarnishing his once untouchable legacy
news_items.summary: On Tuesday, San Francisco’s president of baseball operations addressed the media for the first time since the Pride Night kerfuffle heard ‘round the world. It did not go well.
